name: Herbed Lentil Loaf
recipeIngredient: 2 cups lentils, picked over and rinsed
3 celery ribs with leaves, finely chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 350 degrees.
2. Lightly oil an 8 x 8 baking pan.
3. Put 3 1/2 cups of water and the lentils in a large saucepan. Cover and bring to a boil over medium-high heat. Reduce the heat and simmer until the water is absorbed, about 50 to 60 minutes.
4. Meanwhile, put the tofu, remaining 1 cup water, oats, soy sauce, and pepper in a blender and blend until smooth. Add parsley, basil, thyme, and sage and pulse just to mix. Transfer to a large bowl.
5. Heat olive oil in a large frying pan over medium-high heat. Add celery, onion, and garlic and cook until softened, about 6 minutes. Add to the tofu mixture.
6. Add cooked lentils to the tofu-vegetable mixture and stir to combine. Press the mixture firmly into the prepared pan.
7. Bake for 15 minutes.
8. Meanwhile, put almonds, yeast flakes, and soy sauce in a food processor fitted with the metal blade and pulse until coarsely chopped.
9. Sprinkle topping over lentil loaf and bake for 15 more minutes. Let cool for 10 minutes before cutting into squares. Serve with Mushroom Miso Sauce on the side.
Mushroom Miso Sauce:
1. Put flour in a small dry frying pan over medium heat and toast, stirring often, until darkened evenly to light brown.
2. Transfer flour to a medium bowl.
3. In a small bowl, stir nutritional yeast, miso, and hot water together.
4. In a medium saucepan, heat olive oil over medium heat. Add onion and cook until softened about 5 minutes. Add mushrooms and cook until lightly browned about 5 minutes.
5. Meanwhile, add 2 cups water to the browned flour and whisk until combined.
6. Whisk flour mixture into mushrooms and simmer, whisking often, until thickened, about 1 to 2 minutes. Stir in nutritional yeast and miso mixture. Bring just to a simmer and remove from heat. Serve hot.
